name: Amr ibn Abdullah
title: Devoted Wounded
shortDescription: A companion of the Imam who was severely wounded and fell unconscious on Ashura, and was martyred a year later due to his injuries.
fullStory:
Amr ibn Abdullah al-Junda'i was a loyal and brave companion of Imam Hussain (AS) whose story of sacrifice is similar to 'Sawwar ibn Mun'im'.

On Ashura, Amr fought the enemy with all his might until he sustained multiple blows to his head and body, and fell unconscious on the battlefield due to severe injuries. Ibn Sa'd's forces thought he was killed.

After the battle, his family (or tribesmen) found his half-dead body and secretly took him to Kufa for treatment.

However, the wounds from Karbala were so deep that after a year of enduring pain and suffering, Amr finally attained martyrdom due to those very injuries, and his name was immortalized in the Ziyarat al-Nahiya al-Muqaddasa.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلَى المُرْتَثِّ مَعَهُ، عَمْرِو بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الجُنْدَعِيِّ...
quote: (Ziyarat al-Nahiya:) Peace be upon the one wounded and fallen in the field, Amr ibn Abdullah al-Junda'i...
